text:
In the quiet town of Birchwood, every night was eerily calm. Except for Fredrick, the old recluse who lived topologically off reality. Every week, he'd regale his tiny circle of companions with wild tales about monsters that didn't exist... at least not that you'd want to live next to.

One evening, as the moon painted the sky with shades of lavender, Fredrick shared his latest epic: the Flying Fisherman's Giggling Ghost.

"You see," he began, his voice a gentle drone, "your favorite ghost floats around, spreading laughter. But wait—there's a twist. When you laugh too loud, you turn into a chicken!"

His friends exchanged a mix of amused nods and puzzled glances.

"You chicken!" Fredrick chuckled, waving a phantom hand. "But you can't forget the laugh!"

The room fell silent, all except Fredrick's booming laughter. Nights turned into moons turned into moonslit laughs until it felt like an entire
